modules.description: Top-level wrapper instantiating all submodules and connecting pipeline stages
modules.functionality: Instantiates and interconnects the IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB pipeline stages, register file, ALU, immediate generator, control unit, hazard unit, and four pipeline registers to form a complete RV32I single-issue in-order core. Connects all stage outputs/inputs to pipeline registers and control signals between hazard unit and stages. Provides direct interfaces to instruction and data memories (single-cycle).

modules.description: Instruction fetch stage - generates PC and fetches from IMEM

ports.name: pc_redirect
ports.description: PC redirect enable (from EX for branch, from ID for JAL/JALR)

ports.name: if_pc
ports.description: Current PC sent to IF/ID pipeline register

ports.name: if_instr
ports.description: Fetched instruction sent to IF/ID pipeline register

ports.name: if_valid
ports.description: Instruction valid signal sent to IF/ID pipeline register

modules.functionality: Manages the program counter (PC) to fetch instructions from the instruction memory. On reset, PC is initialized; otherwise, on each cycle without stall, the PC updates to pc_target if pc_redirect is asserted (branches or jumps), or increments by 4. Stalls hold the PC and output; flushes invalidate the current instruction (if_valid = 0). Outputs the current PC, fetched instruction, and a valid flag to the IF/ID register.

modules.description: Instruction decode, register file read, immediate generation

ports.name: id_rs1_addr
ports.description: Source register 1 address (forwarded to hazard unit)

ports.name: id_rs2_addr
ports.description: Source register 2 address (forwarded to hazard unit)

ports.name: id_pc_redirect
ports.description: PC redirect for jumps (JAL/JALR resolved in ID)

modules.functionality: Decodes the instruction from the IF/ID register. Uses the immediate generator (imm_gen) to extract the immediate field and the control unit to produce main control signals. Reads source registers from the register file via rf_rs1_addr/rf_rs2_addr. For JAL and JALR, calculates the target address in this stage and asserts id_pc_redirect to fetch the target. Flushes on taken branches or jumps. Outputs decoded fields, register data, immediate, and control signals to the ID/EX pipeline register.

modules.description: Execute stage - ALU operation, branch resolution

ports.name: fwd_alu_a
ports.description: Forward select for ALU operand A: 00=regfile, 01=MEM, 10=WB

ports.name: fwd_alu_b
ports.description: Forward select for ALU operand B: 00=regfile, 01=MEM, 10=WB

ports.name: ex_alu_result
ports.description: ALU result passed to EX/MEM register

ports.name: ex_mem_wdata
ports.description: Store data (rs2 after forwarding) to EX/MEM

ports.name: ex_reg_write_out
ports.description: Register write enable passed to EX/MEM

modules.functionality: Performs arithmetic and logical operations by sending operands to the ALU. Uses forwarding multiplexers controlled by fwd_alu_a/fwd_alu_b to select among register file data, MEM stage result, and WB stage result for each operand, resolving data hazards. Resolves branch instructions by computing the branch condition (using ALU zero and logic) and target (PC + imm), then asserts branch_taken to the hazard unit. Passes ALU result, store data, destination register, and all control signals to the EX/MEM pipeline register. The stage can be flushed by upstream hazards/branch mispredictions.

modules.description: Memory access stage - load/store via DMEM

ports.name: mem_alu_result
ports.description: ALU result from EX/MEM register (address for load/store)

ports.name: mem_rdata
ports.description: Load data (aligned and sign-extended) to MEM/WB

modules.functionality: Handles all data memory operations. For loads (mem_read=1), sends the ALU result as address to DMEM, captures read data, then performs alignment and sign-extension according to mem_funct3 (LB, LH, LW, LBU, LHU). For stores (mem_write=1), sends address and write data with appropriate byte enable derived from mem_funct3 (SB, SH, SW). The ALU result is passed through to the MEM/WB register alongside the load result, destination register, and control signals for write-back.

modules.description: Write-back stage - writes result to register file
modules.functionality: Selects the final result from either the ALU output, memory load data, or PC+4 based on wb_wb_sel. If wb_reg_write is asserted and the instruction is valid, writes the selected result to the register file at wb_rd_addr. Outputs the write address, data, and enable to the register file, and also forwards the final result to the hazard unit for data forwarding to earlier stages.

modules.description: 32 x 32-bit general purpose register file (x0 hardwired to 0)
modules.functionality: Implements the 32 general-purpose integer registers specified by RV32I. Register x0 is hardwired to zero: reads always return 0, writes to x0 are ignored. Two asynchronous read ports provide rs1_data and rs2_data combinationally from the address on rs1_addr/rs2_addr. The synchronous write port writes wdata to the register at waddr on the rising edge of clk when we is high (and waddr != 0). On reset, all registers (except x0) are cleared to zero.

modules.description: Arithmetic and logic unit for RV32I operations

ports.name: zero
ports.description: Zero flag, asserted when result equals zero

modules.functionality: Performs arithmetic and logical operations on 32-bit operands A and B as selected by op. For shift operations SLL, SRL, SRA only the lower 5 bits of B are used as shift amount. SLT performs signed comparison; SLTU performs unsigned comparison. Outputs the computed result and a zero flag (result == 0). All operations are combinational.

modules.description: Extract and sign-extend immediate from instruction
modules.functionality: Examines the instruction opcode and extracts the immediate field according to the RISC-V specification. For I-type, sign-extends bit 31; for S-type, concatenates upper bits with lower bits; B-type includes zero as LSB; U-type places immediate in upper 20 bits; J-type uses a special encoding. Outputs the fully sign-extended 32-bit immediate value.

modules.description: Decode instruction and generate control signals
modules.functionality: Combinationally decodes the 32-bit RISC-V instruction to generate all datapath control signals. Identifies instruction type (R, I, S, B, U, J) from opcode and funct3/funct7, and produces appropriate ALU operation code, register write enable, memory enable signals, branch/jump flags, and write-back select. Supports all 37 RV32I instructions.

modules.description: Detect hazards and generate stall/flush/forwarding signals

ports.name: branch_taken
ports.description: Branch resolved taken from EX stage

ports.name: fwd_alu_a
ports.description: Forwarding select for ALU input A: 00=regfile, 01=MEM, 10=WB

ports.name: fwd_alu_b
ports.description: Forwarding select for ALU input B: 00=regfile, 01=MEM, 10=WB

ports.name: fwd_mem_result
ports.description: Forwarded data from MEM stage to EX

ports.name: fwd_wb_result
ports.description: Forwarded data from WB stage to EX

modules.functionality: Detects all pipeline hazards and generates appropriate stall and flush signals. For data hazards, compares source register addresses of instructions in ID/EX with destination register addresses of instructions in EX, MEM, and WB. If a later instruction has a pending write to a register needed by an earlier instruction, forwarding is selected (fwd_alu_a/b) using the appropriate MEM or WB result. For load-use hazards (a load instruction in EX followed by an instruction in ID that uses its result), the pipeline is stalled (stall_if, stall_id) for one cycle. For control hazards (branches/jumps), when a branch is taken (branch_taken) or a jump is in ID (jump_taken), instructions in IF and ID are flushed (flush_if, flush_id). Also flushes EX if necessary. The unit also passes the forwarded data values from MEM and WB stages directly to the EX stage.

modules.description: Pipeline register between IF and ID stages
modules.functionality: Captures IF stage outputs (PC, instruction, valid) on the rising edge of clk when stall is deasserted. On reset, outputs are cleared. On flush, the valid bit is cleared, effectively turning the instruction into a NOP. On stall, the register holds its current value, preserving the instruction for the next cycle.

modules.description: Pipeline register between ID and EX stages
modules.functionality: Registers all ID stage outputs (control signals, register addresses/data, immediate, PC, and valid) and makes them available to the EX stage. On reset, clears all registers. On flush, the valid bit is cleared to invalidate the instruction (effectively a bubble). No stall input; stalling is managed in earlier pipeline stages.

modules.description: Pipeline register between EX and MEM stages
modules.functionality: Captures ALU result, store data, destination register, and memory/control signals from the EX stage. On reset, clears outputs. On flush, the valid bit is cleared to insert a bubble. No stall input; data passes on every clock edge (unless flushed).

modules.description: Pipeline register between MEM and WB stages
modules.functionality: Registers the MEM stage outputs (load data, ALU result, destination register, and write-back control signals). On reset, clears registers. On flush, valid is cleared to prevent write-back. Provides synchronous transition of data to the WB stage.
